seoTitle: Beresford vs Bernier Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Beresford and Bernier Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Beresford vs Bernier Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Beresford and Bernier Island.
heading: Beresford vs Bernier Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Beresford, Western Australia and Bernier Island, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Beresford has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bernier Island (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Beresford or Bernier Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Beresford has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Bernier Island scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Beresford scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Bernier Island scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Beresford or Bernier Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Beresford has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
